voice_match_signals: Didactic-corrective register: 'Note carefully: this analog does NOT claim practitioners transcend ontology.' — consistent with 2.41 / 2.42 / 2.44's same explicit corrective marker.

Honest scope-flagging: the verse explicitly self-tags STRETCHED and confidence MEDIUM, matching the chapter's commitment to honest failure-tagging (per the chapter thesis: 'better honest gaps than forced cleverness').
Three-Mondays concrete scenario with named tools (Postgres, Notion, GitHub, Datadog) matches the chapter's scenario-naming convention.
Sanskrit transliterations preserved: nistrai-guṇya, nirdvandva, nitya-sattva-stha, niryoga-kṣema, ātmavān, sattva, rajas, tamas, guṇa.
Quotable line is structural-diagnostic: 'most engineers ship well only inside sattva; the senior this shloka names performs the same baseline across all three Mondays.'
verdict_reason: Voice fits the chapter's register. The willingness to self-tag STRETCHED on the metaphysically loaded verse is itself a chapter-consistent move — 2.46 and 2.48 also operate at the operational layer without overclaiming the metaphysics. PASS.
